title: Today's AI Brief: Cisco and OpenAI redefine enterprise engineering with Codex
summary: Cisco and OpenAI team up with Codex, Pope's AI-assisted writings, and the state of AI in enterprise

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I'm looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Let's dive right in. We've got a couple of stories that caught my eye this week, and I'm excited to share them with you.

First up, Cisco and OpenAI are joining forces with Codex to revolutionize enterprise engineering. Codex is essentially a coding assistant that helps developers write code faster and more efficiently. For Cisco, this means scaling AI-native development, speeding up AI defense work, and automating defect remediation. It's like having a super-smart coding buddy that helps you get the job done quicker and with fewer errors. This partnership is a big deal because it shows how AI is becoming a crucial tool for businesses to stay competitive.

Next, I've got a curious story about the Pope. Apparently, analyses suggest that parts of his recent document, Magnifica Humanitas, were written with the help of AI. Now, I'm not sure if this is a case of AI-assisted writing or AI-driven creativity, but it's certainly raising some eyebrows. The Pope is talking about the dangers of AI, and it's interesting to see if he's also using it to make his point. It's a bit like the pot calling the kettle black, but I suppose that's a topic for another time.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
